input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with Server interactivity

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with Server interactivity

data.reasoningText: The user wants to see what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. I can do a dry run using `dotnet new` with the `--dry-run` flag.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App template with Server interactivity

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App template with Server interactivity", "initial_wait": 15}

data.content:
Here's what `dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server` would generate:

**App structure (~20 source files + Bootstrap assets):**

| Path | Description |
|---|---|
| `Program.cs` | Entry point with server-side Blazor services |
| `MyBlazorApp.csproj` | Project file |
| `appsettings.json` / `appsettings.Development.json` | Configuration |
| `Properties/launchSettings.json` | Dev launch profiles |
| **Components/** | |
| `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor` | Root app scaffolding |
| `Layout/MainLayout.razor(.css)` | Main layout |
| `Layout/NavMenu.razor(.css)` | Sidebar navigation |
| `Layout/ReconnectModal.razor(.css/.js)` | SignalR reconnect UI |
| `Pages/Home.razor` | Home page |
| `Pages/Counter.razor` | Interactive counter demo |
| `Pages/Weather.razor` | Weather data page |
| `Pages/Error.razor`, `Pages/NotFound.razor` | Error handling |
| **wwwroot/** | |
| `app.css`, `favicon.png` | App styles & icon |
| `lib/bootstrap/dist/**` | ~48 Bootstrap CSS/JS files |

A post-creation `dotnet restore` would also run automatically. Say the word if you'd like me to create it.
